external_reference_notes.note: The standard recurrence commonly uses u in [0, 0.5] with coefficients 0.3, 0.05, 1.5, 0.1.
A reservoir-computing tutorial explicitly notes that even standard NARMA10 can occasionally diverge and cites reduced input range u in [0, 0.2] as one documented approach.

hypothesis: A documented finite-stream NARMA10 repair can preserve the nonlinear-memory benchmark role while eliminating long-run non-finite targets across locked lengths and seeds.
mechanism_under_test: benchmark stream generator policy only; no CRA mechanism, model score, readout retune, baseline freeze, or hardware/native transfer is tested here
nonclaims: not evidence that CRA beats ESN/ridge/online baselines
not language, broad reasoning, AGI, or ASI evidence
null_hypothesis: The NARMA10 repair is not finite across locked lengths/seeds, silently changes the benchmark without labeling it, or would allow post-hoc score inflation.

pass_fail_criteria.criterion: selected generator finite at all required length/seed cells
pass_fail_criteria.pass_rule: observed, target, and train-prefix normalized arrays are all finite for 8000/16000/32000 x seeds 42/43/44
the original standard_u05 generator is retained as an audit row and reproduces at least one non-finite long-run cell
the repaired stream must be scored in a new gate, with all models rerun on the same repaired stream and no direct recycling of prior NARMA scores
the repair changes only the benchmark generator policy, not CRA mechanisms, baselines, readout hyperparameters, or splits

question: Can the long-run NARMA10 stream be made finite under a predeclared standardized rule before rerunning any long-run scoreboard?

repair_options.literature_status: standard Atiya/Parlos-style NARMA10 input range used broadly in reservoir-computing benchmarks
repair_options.rationale: Preserve as the original standard-equation audit row; 7.7d showed it can diverge at long length.

repair_options.literature_status: documented NARMA-family divergence-control option; changes target scale and therefore requires a fresh repaired-scoreboard rerun
repair_options.rationale: Keep the same NARMA10 recurrence coefficients and seed policy, but reduce the input range using a documented divergence-control variant. Future scoring must label this as repaired NARMA10 and may not merge it silently with prior u05 scores.
repair_options.role: selected finite-stream repair for the next long-run rerun

repair_options.literature_status: bounded-output wrappers appear in NARMA-family variants but are not selected for this repair
repair_options.rationale: Finite by construction but changes the target nonlinearity more strongly than reduced input range, so it remains a fallback only.

selected_generator_rule.labeling_rule: future scoreboards must label NARMA as repaired_narma10_reduced_input_u02 and must not merge these scores silently with prior u05 NARMA scores
selected_generator_rule.seed_policy: same locked seeds 42/43/44 with the existing seed+3003 random stream
